summary: The package supports one bounded mechanism around ensembling under unstable rollouts, while most other performance claims remain benchmark-specific and not causally decomposed.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The reported 65.4% SWE-bench Verified result belongs to a specific Claude Sonnet 3.7 plus OpenAI o1 system configuration.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence establishes the reported score and system composition, but it does not isolate the causal contribution of each component or justify transfer beyond this exact setup.

bounded_claims.claim_text: Claude Sonnet 3.7 thinking mode did not improve SWE-bench performance.
bounded_claims.bounds: The negative result is supported on this benchmark, but the evidence does not show whether the failure came from task structure, prompt design, cost tradeoffs, or another constraint.

bounded_claims.claim_text: SWE-bench results have limited external validity for broader coding work.
bounded_claims.bounds: The package supports this as a bound, not a full causal account: outcomes are unstable, the benchmark is Python-only, Python may be easier for agents than other languages, and methods or changes that work on SWE-bench do not cleanly map to production settings.
